records.ety: [OE. wo, wa, woo, AS. wā, interj.; akin to D. wee, OS. & OHG. wē, G. weh, Icel. vei, Dan. vee, Sw. ve, Goth. wai; cf. L. vae, Gr. <?/. √128. Cf. Wail.]

quotes: Thus saying, from her side the fatal key, Sad instrument of all our woe, she took.
Can there be a woe or curse in all the stores of vengeance equal to the malignity of such a practice?
O! woe were us alive [i.e., in life].
Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker!
Woe worth the chase, woe worth the day, That costs thy life, my gallant gray!
notes: ☞ Woe is used in denunciation, and in exclamations of sorrow. “ Woe is me! for I am undone.”

quotes: His clerk was woe to do that deed.
Woe was this knight and sorrowfully he sighed.
And looking up he waxed wondrous woe.
